     — CI checks the commits, and commitizen uses them to version + changelog. -->

## How to test

<!-- Steps to verify locally. The fast paths:
     - `make test`  — backend unit tests
     - `make up` then `/smoke` — end-to-end auth + API check -->

## Checklist

- [ ] Commits follow Conventional Commits (`feat:`, `fix:`, `chore:`, …)
- [ ] `make test` passes
- [ ] Docs updated if behavior or setup changed (README / CONTRIBUTING / CHANGELOG)
- [ ] No secrets added to committed files
